item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't understood anything… -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you posted this explanation, I felt dumb after it XD
+1 to the SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolute nonsense. I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a result, actual human death by dysentery appears to have been eliminated entirely.

No-one should be generating reports of deaths from dysentery at all other than the Foundation since no-one is actually dying from it.

Should an independent report submit a verifiable case of death by dysentery, that report is to be added to the sum, and SCP-4946 is to be reclassified as Neutralized.

If someone does die from it, the anomaly has been neutralised.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another thing I don't know if anyone's addressed is what happens if the number of reported deaths exceeds Fibonacci(n).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing. And love the polvo links above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'"SCP-4946 merely represents a sum total of published reports"

I'd change this to "reported deaths". If they had to publish individual reports for every death, containment would become impossible very quickly, and if the anomaly still continues on its own it will end the world by covering it in reports or something. If, however, it's just the amount of deaths they report, it works.

Aside from that, I think it's a pretty cool concept. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Sure is a good thing we aren't seeing people dying from dysentery any more, eh?"

"Have you ever read a medical textbook that's less than 50 years old?"

"… what?"

" 'Dysentery' is an obsolete blanket term. It's deprecated as insufficiently specific. People are dying of shigellosis, of amoebiasis, of E. coli poisoning. I have no idea why this 'Statistical Calamity Prevention' agency is reporting eleven trillion quadrillion octillion 'deaths by dysentery' this week, but they're as full of shit as their imaginary patients."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless I'm misunderstanding something, I don't see how the deception wouldn't be found out fairly quickly by any other group studying or compiling deaths by dysentary. Not necessarily that the anomaly would be discovered, but that research scientists would surely take note that though reputable sources seem to indicate that dysentary deaths have fallen off significantly, there's this one source that keeps publishing increasingly alarming yet unverifiable death tolls, which follow a well known mathematical progression.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This took me a good while to "get" it, but I absolutely love it. Short, sweet, but with a nice twist that really made me think.

I guess they would very quickly be forced to make a random dysentery report generator merely to keep up. Of course after a certain point, the idea of "one death is a tragedy, one million is a statistic" might mean they would just have to report a plausible reason for why these deaths are occurring, and then the overall number.

It's a very silly concept, but it still fits with the tone of the site somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Within 9 months, they'll need to be generating more reports daily than there are atoms making up the planet Earth.

6 months after that, they'll need to be generating more reports daily than there are particles in the universe.

You have died of dysentery reports.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation must do a number of Very Silly Things in pursuit of normalcy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps I still don't understand how this SCP works, but the containment procedures do not seem to be sufficient. My understanding is that the anomaly was causing additional deaths via dysentery such that the reporting on such deaths followed the Fibonacci sequence. Hence by getting ahead of the curve and falsely reporting the deaths as satisfying the sequence no deaths occur at all.

click for over analysis
– hide block

I don't know how the anomaly got off the ground in the first place, as ~1600 people die daily of dysentery worldwide1 and Fibonacci doesn't reach that level till day 172. Going from the dates given, 25 days pass before containment procedures are enacted, which would imply 193,834 deaths by dysentery above the world's baseline. Pretty catastrophic but I guess the article leaves the death toll as an exercise for the reader. The real problem arises in 50 days3 when the number of reported cases passes the population of the Earth4. Given the last line about the necessity to mislead the public, it would seem that the reports need to be public accessible and widely believed to be effective. This will quickly become impossible as the Fibonacci sequence grows exponentially5 leading to the reports becoming obviously ludicrous.

I often like short scps that leave you thinking about the implications long after, and ones that blur the lines between abstraction and reality as well, however this one feels more stuck in my teeth than stuck in my head. I'm probably thinking way too much about what was intended to be a cute Oregon Trail reference. It motivated me to finally make an account after lurking for years, so that's a plus.

Footnotes
1. https://www.geni.com/projects/People-who-died-from-Dysentery/28045
2. $F(17)=1,597$
3. 26/7/2025
4. $F(50)=12,586,269,025$
5. $F(n)\approx e^{\frac{1}{2}(1+\sqrt {5})n}$ for large values of n

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took me a few reads, but once I got it I loved it. Unreserved +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In a sense, it's not only containing itself, but it's also containing dysentery as a whole. That is, if I read the skip right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well the foundation is reporting a ton of fake disentery deaths so that they can match the number they need. In this way, if someone actually died of dysentery there would be more reported than the actual number and they could consider the anomaly neutralized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would suggest adding (additions in italics) "Research into such deaths reveals that, aside from standard statistical error, all recorded deaths before current containment procedures were enacted were the result of otherwise non-anomalous cases of dysentery."

Otherwise, you're saying that deaths are ongoing in the fourth paragraph, and not occuring in the last paragraph.

item.title: Re: anteater potato
item.content:encoded:
That has long fascinated me, chiefly because my grandfather's grandfather took his family on the Oregon Trail in 1840 and didn't lose a single person to dysentery.

There are many unpleasant ways to die, and that is definitely one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may be just me, but following the 4K Contest, I have noticed that there has been a sharp increase of sub par short articles floating around the site and this is no exception. I am fond of the concept thoug, which prevents me from giving this a -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Explanation.
– hide block

The twist is that the total number of reported deaths is equal to Fibonacci(n), which is anomalous behavior. However, since it is off reports, then reporting Fibonacci(n) deaths means that there can not be any more actual deaths that could possibly be reported. Thus, by reporting the deaths, the anomaly is exploited and all deaths by dysentery are prevented around the world. So it's a net positive.

Of course, if they stop reporting false deaths, it's a disaster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fibonacci sequence is an infinite sequence of progressively larger numbers, not a single number.
Specifically, the next term in the sequence is the sum of the previous two terms:

1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ….and so on

Fibonnacci(n) means the nth number in the series. So on day 6 they look at the 6th number in Fibonacci sequence, and get 8. Then tell the world 8 people died of dysentery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes no sense to me. I’m don’t math too good but isn’t the Fibonacci sequence a set number? So you’re multiplying that number by the number of days past this future date? Wouldn’t the report pretty quickly say that everyone has died of dysentery? And if that’s the point, then why? Are you trying to contain the knowledge that dysentery isn’t a thing past that date? Also why?

Honestly, I feel like this is too much confusion for what seems like an “Oregon Trail” reference. Sorry but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have haven't died of dysentery.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone wanna explain why exactly this is Thaumiel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so confused

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure what's going on here. If SCP-4947 doesn't represent the number of actual human deaths by dysentery, but only the number reported, how does filling the numbers with a false report eliminate actual human deaths entirely? There should be plenty which are never directly reported that still occur. Is stating that there are no actual human deaths a false statement designed to not add to the number of deaths reported by Sunny Skies Analytics?

This seems to underestimate how common dysentery is and how huge an undertaking it would be to conceal the disappearance of all dysentery deaths. Is this an intentional undercurrent to the SCP or an oversight?

I personally don't like future SCPs, but this one seems especially bad to me because it's not just supposing an alternate future (with future tech or whatever) but just has to be set at a different time otherwise the core concept of the SCP is easily disprovable.

I actually quite like the idea here and the aspects I find confusing could potentially be as intended (something that appears quite simple but has a lot of strange aspects after thinking about it for a while). However, as it stands it's just confusing to me, not strange-confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This shouldn't be a Thaumiel (what is it helping to contain?) but it's otherwise really great- short, sweet and clever. Easy +1.

item.title: Re: anteater potato
item.content:encoded:
I doubt there was an SCP, but I did find this…

http://www.drlindseyfitzharris.com/2013/07/16/you-have-died-of-dysentery/

Thoughts?

item.title: Re: anteater potato
item.content:encoded:
anteater potato

You are a wordsmith. Oh and the article is good too.
😘

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a supremely clever Thaumiel.

item.title: anteater potato
item.content:encoded:
Tell me your story slow please
Or sing another old time tune
By the light of the moon,
By the light of the moon,
By the light of the moon,
By the light of the moon...

Shoutout to Nicolini for forcing me to write this, and Roget/Seagull for the critique.

This all came about because I had sworn there was an SCP called "You Have Died of Dysentery", except there never was.
